claim: A privacy-preserving longitudinal health benchmark should preserve site boundaries and sampling irregularity before claims are promoted into discovery memory.

missingness.sampling_cadence: irregular visits and uneven measurement windows
missingness.site_specific_absence: site A has imaging; site B has labs; site C has symptom text only
missingness.dropout_or_censoring: follow-up loss differs across sites and may correlate with severity

revision_reason: Randomized data splits can overstate generalization and privacy readiness for real multi-site clinical or animal-health workflows.

site_boundary_model.what_leaves_site: model updates and aggregate metrics only

partition_realism: The split follows real clinic/source boundaries rather than random row-level partitioning, preserving heterogeneity by instrument, cadence, and population.

privacy_utility_tradeoff.expected_privacy_cost: less stable updates when sites are small or missing modalities
privacy_utility_tradeoff.minimum_acceptable_utility: no worse than 5 percentage points below non-private baseline on held-out sites
privacy_utility_tradeoff.stability_risk: privacy noise and non-IID clients may create unstable convergence

falsification_route: Demote the packet if site-aware evaluation performs no better than row-random evaluation or if update leakage cannot be bounded.
next_executable_action: Run validator and add one held-out-site evaluation fixture.
